name: Schema Analyser
description: Analyses the source database schema to produce a full table inventory, FK relationship map, PII/GDPR field classification, and migration complexity scores. All PII field values are scrubbed before the LLM sees actual data — only field names and types are processed.

checks.pass: Schema file found — proceeding with analysis
checks.fail: db/schema.sql not found — cannot analyse schema without source DDL

checks.prompt: Analyse the provided database schema file for migration readiness. You MUST: (1) Produce a complete table inventory with estimated row counts and storage size. (2) Map all FK relationships and identify dependency ordering for migration. (3) Identify ALL PII/GDPR fields: emails, names, IP addresses, payment card numbers, national IDs, dates of birth. Classify each as personal/sensitive/payment under GDPR Article 4. Document them prominently in a dedicated PII Field Map section. (4) Rate each table migration complexity as LOW/MEDIUM/HIGH based on constraint count, row volume, and transformation requirements. NOTE: The PII field map contains only column names and types — actual data values have been scrubbed before this prompt.
checks.pass: Schema analysis complete — table inventory, FK map, and PII field map produced
checks.fail: Schema analysis incomplete — missing required sections
